constructors.name: Vector2i
constructors.summary: Constructs a default-initialized [Vector2i] with all components set to [code]0[/code].

constructors.name: Vector2i
constructors.summary: Constructs a [Vector2i] as a copy of the given [Vector2i].

constructors.name: Vector2i
constructors.summary: Constructs a new [Vector2i] from the given [Vector2] by truncating components' fractional parts (rounding towards zero). For a different behavior consider passing the result of [method Vector2.ceil], [method Vector2.floor] or [method Vector2.round] to this constructor instead.

constructors.name: Vector2i
constructors.summary: Constructs a new [Vector2i] from the given [param x] and [param y].

members.name: Abs
members.summary: Returns a new vector with all components in absolute values (i.e. positive).

members.name: Aspect
members.summary: Returns the aspect ratio of this vector, the ratio of [member x] to [member y].

members.name: Clamp
members.signature: public Vector2i Clamp(Vector2i min, Vector2i max)
members.summary: Returns a new vector with all components clamped between the components of [param min] and [param max], by running [method @GlobalScope.clamp] on each component.

members.name: Clampi
members.signature: public Vector2i Clampi(int min, int max)
members.summary: Returns a new vector with all components clamped between [param min] and [param max], by running [method @GlobalScope.clamp] on each component.

members.name: DistanceSquaredTo
members.summary: Returns the squared [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclidean_distance]Euclidean distance[/url] between this vector and [param to]. This method runs faster than [method distance_to], so prefer it if you need to compare vectors or need the squared distance for some formula.

members.name: DistanceTo
members.summary: Returns the [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclidean_distance]Euclidean distance[/url] between this vector and [param to].

members.name: Length
members.summary: Returns the length (magnitude) of this vector.

members.name: LengthSquared
members.summary: Returns the squared length (squared magnitude) of this vector. This method runs faster than [method length], so prefer it if you need to compare vectors or need the squared distance for some formula.

members.name: Max
members.summary: Returns the component-wise maximum of this and [param with], equivalent to [code]Vector2i(maxi(x, with.x), maxi(y, with.y))[/code].

members.name: MaxAxisIndex
members.summary: Returns the axis of the vector's highest value. See [code]AXIS_*[/code] constants. If all components are equal, this method returns [constant AXIS_X].

members.name: Maxi
members.summary: Returns the component-wise maximum of this and [param with], equivalent to [code]Vector2i(maxi(x, with), maxi(y, with))[/code].

members.name: Min
members.summary: Returns the component-wise minimum of this and [param with], equivalent to [code]Vector2i(mini(x, with.x), mini(y, with.y))[/code].

members.name: MinAxisIndex
members.summary: Returns the axis of the vector's lowest value. See [code]AXIS_*[/code] constants. If all components are equal, this method returns [constant AXIS_Y].

members.name: Mini
members.summary: Returns the component-wise minimum of this and [param with], equivalent to [code]Vector2i(mini(x, with), mini(y, with))[/code].

members.name: Sign
members.summary: Returns a new vector with each component set to [code]1[/code] if it's positive, [code]-1[/code] if it's negative, and [code]0[/code] if it's zero. The result is identical to calling [method @GlobalScope.sign] on each component.

members.name: Snapped
members.summary: Returns a new vector with each component snapped to the closest multiple of the corresponding component in [param step].

members.name: Snappedi
members.summary: Returns a new vector with each component snapped to the closest multiple of [param step].

members.name: X
members.summary: The vector's X component. Also accessible by using the index position [code][0][/code].

members.name: Y
members.summary: The vector's Y component. Also accessible by using the index position [code][1][/code].

values.name: AXIS_X
values.summary: Enumerated value for the X axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].

values.name: AXIS_Y
values.summary: Enumerated value for the Y axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].

constants.name: AXIS_X
constants.summary: Enumerated value for the X axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].

constants.name: AXIS_Y
constants.summary: Enumerated value for the Y axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].

constants.name: ZERO
constants.summary: Zero vector, a vector with all components set to [code]0[/code].

constants.name: ONE
constants.summary: One vector, a vector with all components set to [code]1[/code].

constants.name: MIN
constants.summary: Min vector, a vector with all components equal to [code]INT32_MIN[/code]. Can be used as a negative integer equivalent of [constant Vector2.INF].

constants.name: MAX
constants.summary: Max vector, a vector with all components equal to [code]INT32_MAX[/code]. Can be used as an integer equivalent of [constant Vector2.INF].

constants.name: LEFT
constants.summary: Left unit vector. Represents the direction of left.

constants.name: RIGHT
constants.summary: Right unit vector. Represents the direction of right.

constants.name: UP
constants.summary: Up unit vector. Y is down in 2D, so this vector points -Y.

constants.name: DOWN
constants.summary: Down unit vector. Y is down in 2D, so this vector points +Y.

operators.name: operator !=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the vectors are not equal.

operators.name: operator %
operators.summary: Gets the remainder of each component of the [Vector2i] with the components of the given [Vector2i]. This operation uses truncated division, which is often not desired as it does not work well with negative numbers. Consider using [method @GlobalScope.posmod] instead if you want to handle negative numbers. [codeblock] print(Vector2i(10, -20) % Vector2i(7, 8)) # Prints (3, -4) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator %
operators.summary: Gets the remainder of each component of the [Vector2i] with the given [int]. This operation uses truncated division, which is often not desired as it does not work well with negative numbers. Consider using [method @GlobalScope.posmod] instead if you want to handle negative numbers. [codeblock] print(Vector2i(10, -20) % 7) # Prints (3, -6) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Vector2i] by the components of the given [Vector2i]. [codeblock] print(Vector2i(10, 20) * Vector2i(3, 4)) # Prints (30, 80) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Vector2i] by the given [float]. Returns a [Vector2]. [codeblock] print(Vector2i(10, 15) * 0.9) # Prints (9.0, 13.5) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Vector2i] by the given [int].

operators.name: operator +
operators.summary: Adds each component of the [Vector2i] by the components of the given [Vector2i]. [codeblock] print(Vector2i(10, 20) + Vector2i(3, 4)) # Prints (13, 24) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator -
operators.summary: Subtracts each component of the [Vector2i] by the components of the given [Vector2i]. [codeblock] print(Vector2i(10, 20) - Vector2i(3, 4)) # Prints (7, 16) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator /
operators.summary: Divides each component of the [Vector2i] by the components of the given [Vector2i]. [codeblock] print(Vector2i(10, 20) / Vector2i(2, 5)) # Prints (5, 4) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator /
operators.summary: Divides each component of the [Vector2i] by the given [float]. Returns a [Vector2]. [codeblock] print(Vector2i(1, 2) / 2.5) # Prints (0.4, 0.8) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator /
operators.summary: Divides each component of the [Vector2i] by the given [int].

operators.name: operator <
operators.summary: Compares two [Vector2i] vectors by first checking if the X value of the left vector is less than the X value of the [param right] vector. If the X values are exactly equal, then it repeats this check with the Y values of the two vectors. This operator is useful for sorting vectors.

operators.name: operator <=
operators.summary: Compares two [Vector2i] vectors by first checking if the X value of the left vector is less than or equal to the X value of the [param right] vector. If the X values are exactly equal, then it repeats this check with the Y values of the two vectors. This operator is useful for sorting vectors.

operators.name: operator ==
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the vectors are equal.

operators.name: operator >
operators.summary: Compares two [Vector2i] vectors by first checking if the X value of the left vector is greater than the X value of the [param right] vector. If the X values are exactly equal, then it repeats this check with the Y values of the two vectors. This operator is useful for sorting vectors.

operators.name: operator >=
operators.summary: Compares two [Vector2i] vectors by first checking if the X value of the left vector is greater than or equal to the X value of the [param right] vector. If the X values are exactly equal, then it repeats this check with the Y values of the two vectors. This operator is useful for sorting vectors.

operators.name: operator []
operators.summary: Access vector components using their [param index]. [code]v[0][/code] is equivalent to [code]v.x[/code], and [code]v[1][/code] is equivalent to [code]v.y[/code].

operators.name: operator unary+
operators.summary: Returns the same value as if the [code]+[/code] was not there. Unary [code]+[/code] does nothing, but sometimes it can make your code more readable.

operators.name: operator unary-
operators.summary: Returns the negative value of the [Vector2i]. This is the same as writing [code]Vector2i(-v.x, -v.y)[/code]. This operation flips the direction of the vector while keeping the same magnitude.
